Cpt.Power, 24 May 2026BNro 256GB phones are cheap as from 100 eur used even from 50 eur and is enough for everyone s... more"256 GB is enough for everyone" and then you go on talking about 1 TB phones? 🤔 
 
Anyway, no, 256 GB is not enough for everyone and that's why I bought me a 1 TB card once those 256 GB were full. If I hadn't had a card slot, I would have had to buy an entire phone with more storage to replace a perfectly functioning one and I'd pay 10 times more than I did for the card. A colleague literally bought a new ~1500 € iPhone for that reason just as I was ordering my card for under 1/10 of that. 🤣 
 
And I'll take it to my next phone too. While that person will once again pay 500 € extra for more storage. Maybe you people lack imagination that much, but I've got loads of other potential uses for 500 €. 
 
Why should a headphone jack be upgraded? It's perfect at what it does. And it did get mic and button support for phones some time in the past two decades.  
 
Bluetooth still does not have a way to play lossless 44,1/16 Audio CD quality. It's at the same stage of development it was when I first used it 20 years ago. And there's still potential for stutters, disconnects etc. Nothing like that happening with wired audio. And the BT headphones are e-waste with glued batteries in order to cut a headphone's lifespan by 90 % and keep people buying mediocre headphones instead of buying a nice high-end pair once and enjoy for decades.  
 
BT has its uses, but it can't replace wired audio which is why they should co-exist. Just like internal storage and microSD. You have zero arguments as to why removing those is beneficial, you don't want to use them, you can simply not use them. And you know yourself that their removal is a typical anti-consumer move, we as consumers all just lose because of people like you. 
 
If you're happy with buying phones with a feature set typical of what we had over two decades ago, enjoy that, plenty of choice on the market! 🤷‍♂️ I like having complete modern phones sso will continue doing that. 🍻
This phone is always a hit on this site, but never sells that well 🤣🤣🤣

Xperia is like a blessing curse of the phone at this point. 
 
The blessing part. They kept 3.5mm jack, uSD card ,uninterrupted display experience as well as their two stages shutter. They just don't follow the trend that other manufacturers took away the jack and SD card and the fan would still be happy and defending these decision, while also laughing at Xperia that still have 3.5mm jack and micro SD.  
 
I can still enjoy hi-res music with my wired IEM that cost x6 less and sounds as equal or better than those true wireless that would only last for 2 or 3 years. Only thing that my IEM is missing is the ANC which I don't need it anyway. 
Also not to mention the AF and continuous drive mode on the newer Xperia are so peak. They really come so far from their pre 1,5,10 Xperia. 
 
The cursed part. Sony should have step up their game in camera way before the Xperia 1,5,10 line up. Especially the former superior auto and missing OIS. And when they said that they "listened" to the customer. How many people are asking to get rid the 21:9 screen or remove the Album app and replace with the google photo crap that will ask for the drive backup every two days. The removal of 4K panel is understandable, the price would put this phone that is already niche to a super-niche and would sell even less. But if these features make their niche customer happy then they should keep it. 
 
Xperia at this point seems to be just a playground for Sony engineers. And it just so happens that their toy can still make some side hustle money selling the phone to their niche customer thay would still buy it for $1500.  
 
In the end it is just a smartphone. Some people may like iPhone or Vivo for their instagram-ready photo and better software support. Some people (like me) prefer the Xperia for the natural looking and the manual control of the camera. Just buy whatever phone you like and you will be happy.

It's amazing that even with the survey results, you continue to bash Xperia 1 viii. :) To many people, the beautiful design and its compact size (weight, minimal cam bump) are very important factors too. I'm not concerned whether it takes 30 min or 60 to recharge my phone since it lasts 2 days. Also, for most people, we only need sharp photos from 1x - 10x for 99% of the time. We don't pixel-peep. Overall, the phone will appeal to those people who value a lighter, last clunky phone that offers 99% of what we need, and is beautifully built.
